title: rubus fissipetalus
intro: Rubus fissipetalus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or Himalayan strawberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae). It is a type of bramble that is native to the Himalayan region and is known for its edible fruit and medicinal properties. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Rubus fissipetalus can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used as a topical application for skin conditions such as eczema and acne.
